revision_reason: Cycle 3 (HITL #3 request-changes): replaced q3 with LO3 healthcare proxy case (historical bias answer, mechanism name absent from stem); retagged q4 objective_ref to LO3 (facial recognition example-recall); replaced q5 with LO4 question covering both confirmation bias (data-review stage) and automation bias (retraining stage). Keeps total at 6 MCQ.

questions.objective_ref: Explain what it means for a bias to be 'encoded' into an AI system's training data.
Describe labelling bias — when human annotators attach incorrect or prejudiced tags to training data.
Give at least one concrete real-world example for each encoding mechanism — here, the healthcare spending proxy case illustrating historical bias (mechanism name absent from stem).
Give at least one concrete real-world example for each encoding mechanism — here, the facial recognition case illustrating selection/representation bias (mechanism name absent from stem).

Explain how confirmation bias (9.3) and automation bias (9.5) reinforce the encoding problem, and identify why a feedback loop amplifies bias over successive retrains rather than a one-time entry (LO4 + LO5).
Name the Judgment Framework as a structured approach covered in Topics 9.7-9.9.
